projectDescription: Ruby is a dynamic, object-oriented programming language designed for developer happiness and productivity, featuring elegant syntax, powerful metaprogramming, and a rich standard library.
keyFeatures: Dynamic, object-oriented language with elegant and expressive syntax
Everything is an object — including numbers, strings, and nil
Powerful metaprogramming with open classes and mixins
YJIT (Yet Another JIT) and ZJIT compilers for high-performance execution
Ractors for true parallel execution without shared-state threading concerns
Fibers and async I/O via the Scheduler interface
Rich standard library including HTTP, JSON, YAML, ERB, and more
Garbage collection with incremental and major GC strategies
Prism: a portable, error-tolerant parser for Ruby
Cross-platform support: Linux, macOS, Windows, and WebAssembly
publicApiSurface: String — text manipulation methods (gsub, split, match, format, encode)
Array — ordered collection methods (map, select, reduce, flatten, zip)
Hash — key-value store methods (merge, fetch, transform_keys, each)
Integer / Float / Numeric — arithmetic, conversion, iteration
Regexp — pattern matching and capture groups
IO / File — reading, writing, streaming, file system operations
Enumerable — map, select, reject, each_with_object, group_by, tally
Kernel — puts, print, require, require_relative, pp, lambda, proc
Module / Class — include, extend, prepend, attr_accessor, define_method
Exception / rescue / raise — exception hierarchy and error handling
RubyVM::YJIT — JIT compiler interface and statistics
GC — garbage collector tuning and profiling API
ObjectSpace — object enumeration and memory introspection
Comparable / Enumerable — mixin modules for ordering and collection behavior
